question: You are given ground-truth cortical layer annotations and predicted spatial domain labels for one 10x Visium DLPFC tissue section. Evaluate each prediction column against the ground truth using normalized mutual information, homogeneity, and completeness. Which prediction column has the highest NMI? Return only a JSON object with keys best_method, nmi, homogeneity, completeness, and method_ranking_by_nmi. Round numeric values to three decimals.

skills_tested: Coding, Data Wrangling, Marker Gene Detection, Spatial Statistics
question: Using the expression matrix, spatial coordinates, and domain labels, identify the top marker gene for each domain using a one-vs-rest mean expression difference. For each selected marker gene, compute Moran's I using the 6 nearest spatial neighbors. Return only a JSON object mapping each domain to its top_marker_gene and morans_i value rounded to three decimals.

skills_tested: Coding, Reasoning, Data Wrangling, Clustering Evaluation
question: The ground-truth annotations contain a small noncontinuous domain named rare_immune_islands. Two methods produced predicted spatial domains for the same spots. Compare the methods using global normalized mutual information and also compute recall for the rare_immune_islands domain after optimally matching predicted labels to ground-truth domains. Which method better preserves the rare noncontinuous domain? Return only a JSON object with keys nmi_spatial_smooth_method, nmi_expression_weighted_method, rare_domain_recall_spatial_smooth_method, rare_domain_recall_expression_weighted_method, and better_method_for_rare_domain. Round numeric values to three decimals.
